phase1.explanation:
Wrapper classes convert primitives to objects:

Primitive → Wrapper:
• int → Integer
• double → Double
• boolean → Boolean
• char → Character

Autoboxing: automatic primitive → wrapper
• Integer x = 42;  // compiles

Unboxing: automatic wrapper → primitive
• int y = x;  // compiles

Why use wrappers?
• Collections (ArrayList, HashMap) need objects
• Null values (primitives can't be null)
• Utility methods (parseInt, toString, MAX_VALUE)

Common wrapper methods:
• parseInt(String) — converts String to int
• valueOf(String) — converts String to wrapper
• toString() — converts to String

⚠️ NullPointerException: unboxing a null wrapper causes crash
• Integer x = null;
• int y = x;  // NPE!

phase2.errorMessage:

        at Test.main(Test.java:6)

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Uses autoboxing to create Integer, Double, Boolean objects
2. Uses parseInt() to convert "999" to int
3. Uses Integer.MAX_VALUE and Integer.MIN_VALUE
4. Creates an ArrayList<Integer> and adds 5 numbers using autoboxing
5. Prints all numbers using unboxing
dialogueHints: Integer.MAX_VALUE is a constant for max int
Create ArrayList with ArrayList<Integer> numbers = new ArrayList<>();
